title: Extending MVCC to be serializable, in TLA+
excerpt: In the previous blog post, we saw how a transaction isolation strategy built on multi-version concurrency control (MVCC) does not implement the serializable isolation level. Instead, it implements …
content_html:
In the previous blog post, we saw how a transaction isolation strategy built on multi-version concurrency control (MVCC) does not implement the serializable isolation level. Instead, it implements a weaker isolation level called snapshot isolation. In this post, I’ll discuss how that MVCC model can be extended in order to achieve serializability, based on work published by Michael Cahill, Uwe Röhm, and Alan Fekete.

You can find the model I wrote in the https://github.com/lorin/snapshot-isolation-tla repo, in the SSI module (source, pdf).

A quick note on conventions

In this post, I denote read of x=1 as r[x,1]. This means a transaction read the object x which returned a value of 1. As I mentioned in the previous post, you can imagine a read as being the following SQL statement:

SELECT v FROM obj WHERE k='x';

Similarly, I denote a write of y←2 as w[y,2]. This means a transaction wrote the object y with a value of 2. You can imagine this as:

UPDATE obj SET v=2 WHERE k='y';

Finally, I’ll assume that there’s an initial transaction (T0) which sets the values of all of the objects to 0, and has committed before any other transaction starts.

Background

The SQL isolation levels and phenomena

The ANSI/ISO SQL standard defines four transaction isolation levels: read uncommitted, read committed, repeatable read, and serializable. The standard defines the isolation levels in terms of the phenomena they prevent. For example, the dirty read phenomenon is when one transaction reads a write done by a concurrent transaction that has not yet committed. Phenomena are dangerous because they may violate a software developer’s assumptions about how the database will behave, leading to software that behaves incorrectly.

Problems with the standard and a new isolation level

Berenson et al. noted that the standard’s wording is ambiguous, and of the two possible interpretations of the definitions, one was incorrect (permitting invalid execution histories) and the other was overly strict (proscribing valid execution histories).

The overly strict definition implicitly assumed that concurrency control would be implemented using locking, and this ruled out valid implementations based on alternate schemes, in particular, multi-version concurrency control. They also proposed a new isolation level: snapshot isolation.

Formalizing phenomena and anti-dependencies

In his PhD dissertation work, Adya introduced a new formalization for reasoning about transaction isolation. The formalism is based on a graph of direct dependencies between transactions.

One type of dependency Adya introduced is called an anti-dependency, which is critical to the difference between snapshot isolation and serializable.

An anti-dependency between two concurrent transactions is when one read an object and the other writes the object with a different value, for example:

T1 is said to have an anti-dependency on T2: T1 must come before T2 in a serialization:

In dependency graphs, anti-dependencies are labeled with rw because the transaction which does the read must be sequenced before the transaction that does the write, as shown above.

Adya demonstrated that for an implementation that supports snapshot isolation to generate execution histories that are not serializable, there must be a cycle in the dependency graph that includes an anti-dependency.

Non-serializable execution histories in snapshot isolation

In the paper Making Snapshot Isolation Serializable, Fekete et al. further narrowed the conditions under which snapshot isolation could lead to a non-serializable execution history, by proving the following theorem:

THEOREM 2.1. Suppose H is a multiversion history produced under Snapshot Isolation that is not serializable. Then there is at least one cycle in the serialization graph DSG(H), and we claim that in every cycle there are three consecutive transactions Ti.1, Ti.2, Ti.3 (where it is possible that Ti.1 and Ti.3 are the same transaction) such that Ti.1 and Ti.2 are concurrent, with an edge Ti.1 → Ti.2, and Ti.2 and Ti.3 are concurrent with an edge Ti.2 → Ti.3.

They also note:

By Lemma 2.3, both concurrent edges whose existence is asserted must be anti-dependencies:Ti.1→ Ti.2 and Ti.2→ Ti.3.

This means that one of the following two patterns must always be present in a snapshot isolation history that is not serializable:

Modifying MVCC to avoid non-serializable histories

Cahill et al. proposed a modification to MVCC that can dynamically identify potential problematic transactions that could lead to non-serializable histories, and abort them. By aborting these transactions, the resulting algorithm guarantees serializability.

As Fekete et al. proved, under snapshot isolation, cycles can only occur if there exists a transaction which contains an incoming anti-dependency edge and an outgoing anti-dependency edge, which they call pivot transactions.

Their approach is to identify and abort pivot transactions: if an active transaction contains both an outgoing and an incoming anti-dependency, the transaction is aborted. Note that this is a conservative algorithm: some of the transactions that it aborts may have still resulted in serializable execution histories. But it does guarantee serializability.

Their modification to MVCC involves some additional bookkeeping:

  1. Reads performed by each transaction
  2. Which transactions have outgoing anti-dependencies
  3. Which transactions have incoming anti-dependencies

The tracking of reads is necessary to identify the presence of anti-dependencies, since an anti-dependency always involve a read (outgoing dependency edge) and a write (incoming dependency edge).

Extending our MVCC TLA+ model for serializability

Adding variables

I created a new module called SSI, which stands for Serializable Snapshot Isolation. I extended the MVCC model to add three variables to implement the additional bookkeeping required by the Cahill et al. algorithm. MVCC already tracks which objects are written by each transaction, but we need to now also track reads.

  • rds – which objects are read by which transactions
  • outc – set of transactions that have outbound anti-dependencies
  • inc – set of transactions that have inbound anti-dependencies

TLA+ is untyped (unless you’re using Apalache), but we can represent type information by defining a type invariant (above, called TypeOkS). Defining this is useful both for the reader, and because we can check that this holds with the TLC model checker.

Changes in behavior: new abort opportunities

Here’s how the Next action in MVCC compares to the equivalent in SSI.

Note: Because extending the MVCC module brings all of the MVCC names into scope, I had to create new names for each of the equivalent actions in SSI, I did this by appending an S (e.g., StartTransactionS, DeadlockDetectionS).

In our original MVCC implementation, reads and commits always succeeded. Now, it’s possible for an attempted read or an attempted to commit to result in aborts as well, so we needed an action for this, which I called AbortRdS.

Commits can now also fail, so instead of having a single-step Commit action, we now have a BeginCommit action, which will complete successfully by an EndCommit action, or fail with an abort by the AbortCommit action. Writes can also now abort due to the potential for introducing pivot transactions.

Finding aborts with the model checker

Here’s how I used the TLC model checker to generate witnesses of the new abort behaviors:

Aborted reads

To get the model checker to generate a trace for an aborted read, I defined the following invariant in the MCSSI.tla file:

Then I specified it as an invariant to check in the model checker in the MCSSI.cfg file:

INVARIANT 
    NeverAbortsRead

Because aborted reads can, indeed, happen, the model checker returned an error, with the following error trace:

The resulting trace looks like this, with the red arrows indicating the anti-dependencies.

Aborted commit

Similarly, we can use the model checker to identify scenarios where it a commit would fail, by specifying the following invariant:

The checker finds the following violation of that invariant:

While T2 is in the process of committing, T1 performs a read which turns T2 into a pivot transaction. This results in T2 aborting.

Checking serializability using refinement mapping

Just like we did previously with MVCC, we can define a refinement mapping from our SSI spec to our Serializability spec. You can find it in the SSIRefinement module (source, pdf). It’s almost identical to the MVCCRefinement module (source, pdf), with some minor modifications to handle the new abort scenarios.

The main difference is that now the refinement mapping should actually hold, because SSI ensures serializability! I wasn’t able to find a counterexample when I ran the model checker against the refinement mapping, so that gave me some confidence in my model. Of course, that doesn’t prove that my implementation is correct. But it’s good enough for a learning exercise.

Coda: on extending TLA+ specifications

Serializable Snapshot Isolation provides us with a nice example of when we can extend an existing specification rather than create a new one from scratch.

Even so, it’s still a fair amount of work to extend an existing specification. I suspect it would have been less work to take a copy-paste-and-modify approach rather than extending it. Still, I found it a useful exercise in learning how to modify a specification by extending it.

Instead, it implements a weaker isolation level called snapshot isolation. In this post, I’ll discuss how that MVCC model can be extended in order to achieve serializability, based on work published by Michael Cahill, Uwe Röhm, and Alan Fekete. You can find the model I wrote in the https://github.com/lorin/snapshot-isolation-tla repo, in the SSI module (source, pdf). A quick note on conventions In this post, I denote read of x=1 as r[x,1]. This means a transaction read the object x which returned a value of 1. As I mentioned in the previous post, you can imagine a read as being the following SQL statement: SELECT v FROM obj WHERE k='x'; Similarly, I denote a write of y←2 as w[y,2]. This means a transaction wrote the object y with a value of 2. You can imagine this as: UPDATE obj SET v=2 WHERE k='y'; Finally, I’ll assume that there’s an initial transaction (T0) which sets the values of all of the objects to 0, and has committed before any other transaction starts. We assume this transaction always precedes all other transactions Background The SQL isolation levels and phenomena The ANSI/ISO SQL standard defines four transaction isolation levels: read uncommitted, read committed, repeatable read, and serializable. The standard defines the isolation levels in terms of the phenomena they prevent. For example, the dirty read phenomenon is when one transaction reads a write done by a concurrent transaction that has not yet committed. Phenomena are dangerous because they may violate a software developer’s assumptions about how the database will behave, leading to software that behaves incorrectly. Problems with the standard and a new isolation level Berenson et al. noted that the standard’s wording is ambiguous, and of the two possible interpretations of the definitions, one was incorrect (permitting invalid execution histories) and the other was overly strict (proscribing valid execution histories). The overly strict definition implicitly assumed that concurrency control would be implemented using locking, and this ruled out valid implementations based on alternate schemes, in particular, multi-version concurrency control. They also proposed a new isolation level: snapshot isolation. Formalizing phenomena and anti-dependencies In his PhD dissertation work, Adya introduced a new formalization for reasoning about transaction isolation. The formalism is based on a graph of direct dependencies between transactions. One type of dependency Adya introduced is called an anti-dependency, which is critical to the difference between snapshot isolation and serializable. An anti-dependency between two concurrent transactions is when one read an object and the other writes the object with a different value, for example: T1 is said to have an anti-dependency on T2: T1 must come before T2 in a serialization: If T2 is sequenced before T1, then the read will not match the most recent write. Therefore, T1 must come before T2. In dependency graphs, anti-dependencies are labeled with rw because the transaction which does the read must be sequenced before the transaction that does the write, as shown above. Adya demonstrated that for an implementation that supports snapshot isolation to generate execution histories that are not serializable, there must be a cycle in the dependency graph that includes an anti-dependency. Non-serializable execution histories in snapshot isolation In the paper Making Snapshot Isolation Serializable, Fekete et al. further narrowed the conditions under which snapshot isolation could lead to a non-serializable execution history, by proving the following theorem: THEOREM 2.1. Suppose H is a multiversion history produced under Snapshot Isolation that is not serializable. Then there is at least one cycle in the serialization graph DSG(H), and we claim that in every cycle there are three consecutive transactions Ti.1, Ti.2, Ti.3 (where it is possible that Ti.1 and Ti.3 are the same transaction) such that Ti.1 and Ti.2 are concurrent, with an edge Ti.1 → Ti.2, and Ti.2 and Ti.3 are concurrent with an edge Ti.2 → Ti.3. They also note: By Lemma 2.3, both concurrent edges whose existence is asserted must be anti-dependencies:Ti.1→ Ti.2 and Ti.2→ Ti.3. This means that one of the following two patterns must always be present in a snapshot isolation history that is not serializable: Non-serializable snapshot isolation histories must contain one of these as subgraphs in the dependency graph Modifying MVCC to avoid non-serializable histories Cahill et al. proposed a modification to MVCC that can dynamically identify potential problematic transactions that could lead to non-serializable histories, and abort them. By aborting these transactions, the resulting algorithm guarantees serializability. As Fekete et al. proved, under snapshot isolation, cycles can only occur if there exists a transaction which contains an incoming anti-dependency edge and an outgoing anti-dependency edge, which they call pivot transactions. Pivot transactions shown in red Their approach is to identify and abort pivot transactions: if an active transaction contains both an outgoing and an incoming anti-dependency, the transaction is aborted. Note that this is a conservative algorithm: some of the transactions that it aborts may have still resulted in serializable execution histories. But it does guarantee serializability. Their modification to MVCC involves some additional bookkeeping: Reads performed by each transaction Which transactions have outgoing anti-dependencies Which transactions have incoming anti-dependencies The tracking of reads is necessary to identify the presence of anti-dependencies, since an anti-dependency always involve a read (outgoing dependency edge) and a write (incoming dependency edge). Extending our MVCC TLA+ model for serializability Adding variables I created a new module called SSI, which stands for Serializable Snapshot Isolation. I extended the MVCC model to add three variables to implement the additional bookkeeping required by the Cahill et al. algorithm. MVCC already tracks which objects are written by each transaction, but we need to now also track reads. rds – which objects are read by which transactions outc – set of transactions that have outbound anti-dependencies inc – set of transactions that have inbound anti-dependencies TLA+ is untyped (unless you’re using Apalache), but we can represent type information by defining a type invariant (above, called TypeOkS). Defining this is useful both for the reader, and because we can check that this holds with the TLC model checker. Changes in behavior: new abort opportunities Here’s how the Next action in MVCC compares to the equivalent in SSI. Note: Because extending the MVCC module brings all of the MVCC names into scope, I had to create new names for each of the equivalent actions in SSI, I did this by appending an S (e.g., StartTransactionS, DeadlockDetectionS). In our original MVCC implementation, reads and commits always succeeded. Now, it’s possible for an attempted read or an attempted to commit to result in aborts as well, so we needed an action for this, which I called AbortRdS. Commits can now also fail, so instead of having a single-step Commit action, we now have a BeginCommit action, which will complete successfully by an EndCommit action, or fail with an abort by the AbortCommit action. Writes can also now abort due to the potential for introducing pivot transactions. Finding aborts with the model checker Here’s how I used the TLC model checker to generate witnesses of the new abort behaviors: Aborted reads To get the model checker to generate a trace for an aborted read, I defined the following invariant in the MCSSI.tla file: Then I specified it as an invariant to check in the model checker in the MCSSI.cfg file: INVARIANT NeverAbortsRead Because aborted reads can, indeed, happen, the model checker returned an error, with the following error trace: The resulting trace looks like this, with the red arrows indicating the anti-dependencies. Aborted commit Similarly, we can use the model checker to identify scenarios where it a commit would fail, by specifying the following invariant: The checker finds the following violation of that invariant: While T2 is in the process of committing, T1 performs a read which turns T2 into a pivot transaction. This results in T2 aborting. Checking serializability using refinement mapping Just like we did previously with MVCC, we can define a refinement mapping from our SSI spec to our Serializability spec. You can find it in the SSIRefinement module (source, pdf). It’s almost identical to the MVCCRefinement module (source, pdf), with some minor modifications to handle the new abort scenarios. The main difference is that now the refinement mapping should actually hold, because SSI ensures serializability! I wasn’t able to find a counterexample when I ran the model checker against the refinement mapping, so that gave me some confidence in my model. Of course, that doesn’t prove that my implementation is correct. But it’s good enough for a learning exercise. Coda: on extending TLA+ specifications Serializable Snapshot Isolation provides us with a nice example of when we can extend an existing specification rather than create a new one from scratch. Even so, it’s still a fair amount of work to extend an existing specification. I suspect it would have been less work to take a copy-paste-and-modify approach rather than extending it. Still, I found it a useful exercise in learning how to modify a specification by extending it.

Instead, it implements a weaker isolation level called snapshot isolation. In this post, I’ll discuss how that MVCC model can be extended in order to achieve serializability, based on work published by Michael Cahill, Uwe Röhm, and Alan Fekete.

sentences.zh: 相反，它实现了一个较弱的隔离级别，称为快照隔离。

sentences.zh: SQL隔离级别和现象

blocks.html_en: The ANSI/ISO SQL standard defines four transaction isolation levels: read uncommitted, read committed, repeatable read, and serializable. The standard defines the isolation levels in terms of the phenomena they prevent. For example, the dirty read phenomenon is when one transaction reads a write done by a concurrent transaction that has not yet committed. Phenomena are dangerous because they may violate a software developer’s assumptions about how the database will behave, leading to software that behaves incorrectly.

sentences.en: The ANSI/ISO SQL standard defines four transaction isolation levels: read uncommitted, read committed, repeatable read, and serializable.
sentences.zh: ANSI/ISO SQL 标准定义了四种事务隔离级别：未提交读、已提交读、可重复读和可序列化。

sentences.en: The standard defines the isolation levels in terms of the phenomena they prevent.
sentences.zh: 该标准根据隔离级别所防止的现象来定义隔离级别。

sentences.en: For example, the dirty read phenomenon is when one transaction reads a write done by a concurrent transaction that has not yet committed.
sentences.zh: 例如，脏读现象是指一个事务读取了并发事务尚未提交的写操作。

sentences.en: Phenomena are dangerous because they may violate a software developer’s assumptions about how the database will behave, leading to software that behaves incorrectly.
sentences.zh: 现象是危险的，因为它们可能违反软件开发人员对数据库行为方式的假设，导致软件行为不正确。

blocks.html_en: Problems with the standard and a new isolation level

sentences.en: Problems with the standard and a new isolation level
sentences.zh: 标准和新隔离级别的问题

blocks.html_en: Berenson et al. noted that the standard’s wording is ambiguous, and of the two possible interpretations of the definitions, one was incorrect (permitting invalid execution histories) and the other was overly strict (proscribing valid execution histories).

sentences.en: Berenson et al. noted that the standard’s wording is ambiguous, and of the two possible interpretations of the definitions, one was incorrect (permitting invalid execution histories) and the other was overly strict (proscribing valid execution histories).
sentences.zh: 贝伦森等人。指出该标准的措辞含糊不清，并且在定义的两种可能解释中，一种是不正确的（允许无效的执行历史），另一种过于严格（禁止有效的执行历史）。

blocks.html_en: The overly strict definition implicitly assumed that concurrency control would be implemented using locking, and this ruled out valid implementations based on alternate schemes, in particular, multi-version concurrency control. They also proposed a new isolation level: snapshot isolation.

sentences.en: The overly strict definition implicitly assumed that concurrency control would be implemented using locking, and this ruled out valid implementations based on alternate schemes, in particular, multi-version concurrency control.
sentences.zh: 过于严格的定义隐含地假设并发控制将使用锁定来实现，这排除了基于替代方案的有效实现，特别是多版本并发控制。

sentences.en: They also proposed a new isolation level: snapshot isolation.
sentences.zh: 他们还提出了一种新的隔离级别：快照隔离。

sentences.zh: 形式主义基于事务之间的直接依赖关系图。

sentences.zh: 如果 T2 排在 T1 之前，则读取将与最近的写入不匹配。

sentences.en: Therefore, T1 must come before T2.
sentences.zh: 因此，T1 必须先于 T2。

blocks.html_en: In dependency graphs, anti-dependencies are labeled with rw because the transaction which does the read must be sequenced before the transaction that does the write, as shown above.

sentences.en: In dependency graphs, anti-dependencies are labeled with rw because the transaction which does the read must be sequenced before the transaction that does the write, as shown above.
sentences.zh: 在依赖图中，反依赖关系用 rw 标记，因为执行读取的事务必须在执行写入的事务之前排序，如上所示。

blocks.html_en: Adya demonstrated that for an implementation that supports snapshot isolation to generate execution histories that are not serializable, there must be a cycle in the dependency graph that includes an anti-dependency.

sentences.en: Adya demonstrated that for an implementation that supports snapshot isolation to generate execution histories that are not serializable, there must be a cycle in the dependency graph that includes an anti-dependency.
sentences.zh: Adya 证明，对于支持快照隔离以生成不可序列化的执行历史记录的实现，依赖关系图中必须存在包含反依赖关系的循环。

blocks.html_en: Non-serializable execution histories in snapshot isolation

sentences.en: Non-serializable execution histories in snapshot isolation
sentences.zh: 快照隔离中的不可序列化执行历史

blocks.html_en: In the paper Making Snapshot Isolation Serializable, Fekete et al. further narrowed the conditions under which snapshot isolation could lead to a non-serializable execution history, by proving the following theorem:

blocks.html_en: THEOREM 2.1. Suppose H is a multiversion history produced under Snapshot Isolation that is not serializable. Then there is at least one cycle in the serialization graph DSG(H), and we claim that in every cycle there are three consecutive transactions Ti.1, Ti.2, Ti.3 (where it is possible that Ti.1 and Ti.3 are the same transaction) such that Ti.1 and Ti.2 are concurrent, with an edge Ti.1 → Ti.2, and Ti.2 and Ti.3 are concurrent with an edge Ti.2 → Ti.3.

sentences.en: THEOREM 2.1.
Suppose H is a multiversion history produced under Snapshot Isolation that is not serializable.
sentences.zh: 假设 H 是在快照隔离下生成的不可序列化的多版本历史记录。

sentences.en: Then there is at least one cycle in the serialization graph DSG(H), and we claim that in every cycle there are three consecutive transactions Ti.1, Ti.2, Ti.3 (where it is possible that Ti.1 and Ti.3 are the same transaction) such that Ti.1 and Ti.2 are concurrent, with an edge Ti.1 → Ti.2, and Ti.2 and Ti.3 are concurrent with an edge Ti.2 → Ti.3.
sentences.zh: 那么序列化图 DSG(H) 中至少有一个周期，并且我们声称在每个周期中存在三个连续的事务 Ti.1、Ti.2、Ti.3（其中 Ti.1 和 Ti.3 可能是同一个事务），使得 Ti.1 和 Ti.2 是并发的，边有 Ti.1 → Ti.2，并且 Ti.2 和 Ti.3 并发有边 Ti.2 → Ti.3。

blocks.html_en: THEOREM 2.1. Suppose H is a multiversion history produced under Snapshot Isolation that is not serializable. Then there is at least one cycle in the serialization graph DSG(H), and we claim that in every cycle there are three consecutive transactions Ti.1, Ti.2, Ti.3 (where it is possible that Ti.1 and Ti.3 are the same transaction) such that Ti.1 and Ti.2 are concurrent, with an edge Ti.1 → Ti.2, and Ti.2 and Ti.3 are concurrent with an edge Ti.2 → Ti.3.

sentences.en: THEOREM 2.1.
Suppose H is a multiversion history produced under Snapshot Isolation that is not serializable.
sentences.zh: 假设 H 是在快照隔离下生成的不可序列化的多版本历史记录。

sentences.en: Then there is at least one cycle in the serialization graph DSG(H), and we claim that in every cycle there are three consecutive transactions Ti.1, Ti.2, Ti.3 (where it is possible that Ti.1 and Ti.3 are the same transaction) such that Ti.1 and Ti.2 are concurrent, with an edge Ti.1 → Ti.2, and Ti.2 and Ti.3 are concurrent with an edge Ti.2 → Ti.3.
sentences.zh: 那么序列化图 DSG(H) 中至少有一个周期，并且我们声称在每个周期中存在三个连续的事务 Ti.1、Ti.2、Ti.3（其中 Ti.1 和 Ti.3 可能是同一个事务），使得 Ti.1 和 Ti.2 是并发的，边有 Ti.1 → Ti.2，并且 Ti.2 和 Ti.3 并发有边 Ti.2 → Ti.3。

blocks.html_en: They also note:

sentences.en: They also note:
sentences.zh: 他们还指出：

blocks.html_en: By Lemma 2.3, both concurrent edges whose existence is asserted must be anti-dependencies:Ti.1→ Ti.2 and Ti.2→ Ti.3.

sentences.en: By Lemma 2.3, both concurrent edges whose existence is asserted must be anti-dependencies:Ti.1→ Ti.2 and Ti.2→ Ti.3.
sentences.zh: 根据引理 2.3，断言存在的两个并发边必须是反依赖关系：Ti.1→Ti.2 和 Ti.2→Ti.3。

blocks.html_en: By Lemma 2.3, both concurrent edges whose existence is asserted must be anti-dependencies:Ti.1→ Ti.2 and Ti.2→ Ti.3.

sentences.en: By Lemma 2.3, both concurrent edges whose existence is asserted must be anti-dependencies:Ti.1→ Ti.2 and Ti.2→ Ti.3.
sentences.zh: 根据引理 2.3，断言存在的两个并发边必须是反依赖关系：Ti.1→Ti.2 和 Ti.2→Ti.3。

blocks.html_en: This means that one of the following two patterns must always be present in a snapshot isolation history that is not serializable:

sentences.en: This means that one of the following two patterns must always be present in a snapshot isolation history that is not serializable:
sentences.zh: 这意味着以下两种模式之一必须始终存在于不可序列化的快照隔离历史记录中：

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Non-serializable snapshot isolation histories must contain one of these as subgraphs in the dependency graph</figcaption>

sentences.en: Non-serializable snapshot isolation histories must contain one of these as subgraphs in the dependency graph
sentences.zh: 不可序列化的快照隔离历史必须包含其中之一作为依赖关系图中的子图

blocks.html_en: Modifying MVCC to avoid non-serializable histories

sentences.en: Modifying MVCC to avoid non-serializable histories
sentences.zh: 修改 MVCC 以避免不可序列化的历史记录

blocks.html_en: Cahill et al. proposed a modification to MVCC that can dynamically identify potential problematic transactions that could lead to non-serializable histories, and abort them. By aborting these transactions, the resulting algorithm guarantees serializability.

sentences.en: Cahill et al. proposed a modification to MVCC that can dynamically identify potential problematic transactions that could lead to non-serializable histories, and abort them.
sentences.zh: 卡希尔等人。提出了对 MVCC 的修改，可以动态识别可能导致不可序列化历史记录的潜在问题事务，并中止它们。

sentences.en: By aborting these transactions, the resulting algorithm guarantees serializability.
sentences.zh: 通过中止这些事务，生成的算法保证了可串行化。

blocks.html_en: As Fekete et al. proved, under snapshot isolation, cycles can only occur if there exists a transaction which contains an incoming anti-dependency edge and an outgoing anti-dependency edge, which they call pivot transactions.

sentences.en: As Fekete et al. proved, under snapshot isolation, cycles can only occur if there exists a transaction which contains an incoming anti-dependency edge and an outgoing anti-dependency edge, which they call pivot transactions.
sentences.zh: 正如 Fekete 等人。事实证明，在快照隔离下，只有存在包含传入反依赖边缘和传出反依赖边缘的事务（他们称之为枢轴事务）时，才会发生循环。

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Pivot transactions shown in red</figcaption>

sentences.en: Pivot transactions shown in red
sentences.zh: 枢轴交易以红色显示

blocks.html_en: Their approach is to identify and abort pivot transactions: if an active transaction contains both an outgoing and an incoming anti-dependency, the transaction is aborted. Note that this is a conservative algorithm: some of the transactions that it aborts may have still resulted in serializable execution histories. But it does guarantee serializability.

sentences.en: Their approach is to identify and abort pivot transactions: if an active transaction contains both an outgoing and an incoming anti-dependency, the transaction is aborted.
sentences.zh: 他们的方法是识别并中止枢轴事务：如果活动事务同时包含传出和传入反依赖项，则该事务将被中止。

sentences.en: Note that this is a conservative algorithm: some of the transactions that it aborts may have still resulted in serializable execution histories.
sentences.zh: 请注意，这是一个保守的算法：它中止的一些事务可能仍然导致可序列化的执行历史记录。

sentences.en: But it does guarantee serializability.
sentences.zh: 但它确实保证了可串行性。

blocks.html_en: Their modification to MVCC involves some additional bookkeeping:

sentences.en: Their modification to MVCC involves some additional bookkeeping:
sentences.zh: 他们对 MVCC 的修改涉及一些额外的簿记：

blocks.html_en: • Reads performed by each transaction

sentences.en: Reads performed by each transaction
sentences.zh: 每个事务执行的读取

blocks.html_en: • Which transactions have outgoing anti-dependencies

sentences.en: Which transactions have outgoing anti-dependencies
sentences.zh: 哪些交易具有传出反依赖关系

blocks.html_en: • Which transactions have incoming anti-dependencies

sentences.en: Which transactions have incoming anti-dependencies
sentences.zh: 哪些交易具有传入反依赖关系

blocks.html_en: The tracking of reads is necessary to identify the presence of anti-dependencies, since an anti-dependency always involve a read (outgoing dependency edge) and a write (incoming dependency edge).

sentences.en: The tracking of reads is necessary to identify the presence of anti-dependencies, since an anti-dependency always involve a read (outgoing dependency edge) and a write (incoming dependency edge).
sentences.zh: 读取的跟踪对于识别反依赖性的存在是必要的，因为反依赖性总是涉及读取（传出依赖性边缘）和写入（传入依赖性边缘）。

blocks.html_en: Extending our MVCC TLA+ model for serializability

sentences.en: Extending our MVCC TLA+ model for serializability
sentences.zh: 扩展我们的 MVCC TLA+ 模型以实现可序列化

blocks.html_en: Adding variables

sentences.en: Adding variables
sentences.zh: 添加变量

blocks.html_en: I created a new module called SSI, which stands for Serializable Snapshot Isolation. I extended the MVCC model to add three variables to implement the additional bookkeeping required by the Cahill et al. algorithm. MVCC already tracks which objects are written by each transaction, but we need to now also track reads.

sentences.en: I created a new module called SSI, which stands for Serializable Snapshot Isolation.
sentences.zh: 我创建了一个名为 SSI 的新模块，它代表可串行化快照隔离。

sentences.en: I extended the MVCC model to add three variables to implement the additional bookkeeping required by the Cahill et al. algorithm.
sentences.zh: 我扩展了 MVCC 模型，添加了三个变量，以实现 Cahill 等人要求的额外记账功能。算法。

sentences.en: MVCC already tracks which objects are written by each transaction, but we need to now also track reads.
sentences.zh: MVCC 已经跟踪每个事务写入了哪些对象，但我们现在还需要跟踪读取。

blocks.html_en: • rds – which objects are read by which transactions

sentences.en: rds – which objects are read by which transactions
sentences.zh: rds – 哪些事务读取哪些对象

blocks.html_en: • outc – set of transactions that have outbound anti-dependencies

sentences.en: outc – set of transactions that have outbound anti-dependencies
sentences.zh: outc – 具有出站反依赖性的事务集

blocks.html_en: • inc – set of transactions that have inbound anti-dependencies

sentences.en: inc – set of transactions that have inbound anti-dependencies
sentences.zh: inc – 具有入站反依赖性的事务集

blocks.html_en: TLA+ is untyped (unless you’re using Apalache), but we can represent type information by defining a type invariant (above, called TypeOkS). Defining this is useful both for the reader, and because we can check that this holds with the TLC model checker.

sentences.en: TLA+ is untyped (unless you’re using Apalache), but we can represent type information by defining a type invariant (above, called TypeOkS).
sentences.zh: TLA+ 是无类型的（除非您使用 Apalache），但我们可以通过定义类型不变量（上面称为 TypeOkS）来表示类型信息。

sentences.en: Defining this is useful both for the reader, and because we can check that this holds with the TLC model checker.
sentences.zh: 定义它对于读者来说很有用，而且因为我们可以使用 TLC 模型检查器来检查它是否成立。

blocks.html_en: Changes in behavior: new abort opportunities

sentences.en: Changes in behavior: new abort opportunities
sentences.zh: 行为变化：新的中止机会

blocks.html_en: Here’s how the Next action in MVCC compares to the equivalent in SSI.

sentences.en: Here’s how the Next action in MVCC compares to the equivalent in SSI.
sentences.zh: 以下是 MVCC 中的 Next 操作与 SSI 中的等效操作的比较。

blocks.html_en: Note: Because extending the MVCC module brings all of the MVCC names into scope, I had to create new names for each of the equivalent actions in SSI, I did this by appending an S (e.g., StartTransactionS, DeadlockDetectionS).

sentences.en: Note: Because extending the MVCC module brings all of the MVCC names into scope, I had to create new names for each of the equivalent actions in SSI, I did this by appending an S (e.g., StartTransactionS, DeadlockDetectionS).
sentences.zh: 注意：由于扩展 MVCC 模块会将所有 MVCC 名称纳入范围，因此我必须为 SSI 中的每个等效操作创建新名称，我通过附加 S（例如 StartTransactionS、DeadlockDetectionS）来完成此操作。

blocks.html_en: In our original MVCC implementation, reads and commits always succeeded. Now, it’s possible for an attempted read or an attempted to commit to result in aborts as well, so we needed an action for this, which I called AbortRdS.

sentences.en: In our original MVCC implementation, reads and commits always succeeded.
sentences.zh: 在我们最初的 MVCC 实现中，读取和提交总是成功的。

sentences.en: Now, it’s possible for an attempted read or an attempted to commit to result in aborts as well, so we needed an action for this, which I called AbortRdS.
sentences.zh: 现在，尝试读取或尝试提交也可能导致中止，因此我们需要为此采取一项操作，我将其称为 AbortRdS。

blocks.html_en: Commits can now also fail, so instead of having a single-step Commit action, we now have a BeginCommit action, which will complete successfully by an EndCommit action, or fail with an abort by the AbortCommit action. Writes can also now abort due to the potential for introducing pivot transactions.

sentences.en: Commits can now also fail, so instead of having a single-step Commit action, we now have a BeginCommit action, which will complete successfully by an EndCommit action, or fail with an abort by the AbortCommit action.
sentences.zh: 提交现在也可能会失败，因此我们现在不再采用单步 Commit 操作，而是采用 BeginCommit 操作，该操作将通过 EndCommit 操作成功完成，或者通过 AbortCommit 操作中止而失败。

sentences.en: Writes can also now abort due to the potential for introducing pivot transactions.
sentences.zh: 由于可能引入枢轴事务，写入现在也可以中止。

blocks.html_en: Finding aborts with the model checker

sentences.en: Finding aborts with the model checker
sentences.zh: 使用模型检查器查找中止

blocks.html_en: Here’s how I used the TLC model checker to generate witnesses of the new abort behaviors:

sentences.en: Here’s how I used the TLC model checker to generate witnesses of the new abort behaviors:
sentences.zh: 以下是我如何使用 TLC 模型检查器生成新中止行为的见证：

blocks.html_en: Aborted reads

sentences.en: Aborted reads
sentences.zh: 中止读取

blocks.html_en: To get the model checker to generate a trace for an aborted read, I defined the following invariant in the MCSSI.tla file:

sentences.en: To get the model checker to generate a trace for an aborted read, I defined the following invariant in the MCSSI.tla file:
sentences.zh: 为了让模型检查器生成中止读取的跟踪，我在 MCSSI.tla 文件中定义了以下不变量：

blocks.html_en: Then I specified it as an invariant to check in the model checker in the MCSSI.cfg file:

sentences.en: Then I specified it as an invariant to check in the model checker in the MCSSI.cfg file:
sentences.zh: 然后我将其指定为不变量以检查 MCSSI.cfg 文件中的模型检查器：

blocks.html_en: Because aborted reads can, indeed, happen, the model checker returned an error, with the following error trace:

sentences.en: Because aborted reads can, indeed, happen, the model checker returned an error, with the following error trace:
sentences.zh: 由于中止读取确实可能发生，因此模型检查器返回错误，并显示以下错误跟踪：

blocks.html_en: The resulting trace looks like this, with the red arrows indicating the anti-dependencies.

sentences.en: The resulting trace looks like this, with the red arrows indicating the anti-dependencies.
sentences.zh: 生成的跟踪如下所示，其中红色箭头表示反依赖关系。

blocks.html_en: Aborted commit

sentences.en: Aborted commit
sentences.zh: 中止提交

blocks.html_en: Similarly, we can use the model checker to identify scenarios where it a commit would fail, by specifying the following invariant:

sentences.en: Similarly, we can use the model checker to identify scenarios where it a commit would fail, by specifying the following invariant:
sentences.zh: 类似地，我们可以通过指定以下不变量，使用模型检查器来识别提交失败的场景：

blocks.html_en: The checker finds the following violation of that invariant:

sentences.en: The checker finds the following violation of that invariant:
sentences.zh: 检查器发现以下违反该不变量的情况：

blocks.html_en: While T2 is in the process of committing, T1 performs a read which turns T2 into a pivot transaction. This results in T2 aborting.

sentences.en: While T2 is in the process of committing, T1 performs a read which turns T2 into a pivot transaction.
sentences.zh: 当 T2 正在提交过程中时，T1 执行读取操作，将 T2 转变为枢轴事务。

sentences.en: This results in T2 aborting.
sentences.zh: 这会导致 T2 中止。

blocks.html_en: Checking serializability using refinement mapping

sentences.en: Checking serializability using refinement mapping
sentences.zh: 使用细化映射检查可串行性

blocks.html_en: Just like we did previously with MVCC, we can define a refinement mapping from our SSI spec to our Serializability spec. You can find it in the SSIRefinement module (source, pdf). It’s almost identical to the MVCCRefinement module (source, pdf), with some minor modifications to handle the new abort scenarios.

sentences.en: Just like we did previously with MVCC, we can define a refinement mapping from our SSI spec to our Serializability spec.
sentences.zh: 就像我们之前对 MVCC 所做的那样，我们可以定义从 SSI 规范到可串行化规范的细化映射。

sentences.en: You can find it in the SSIRefinement module (source, pdf).
sentences.zh: 您可以在 SSIRefinement 模块中找到它（源代码，pdf）。

sentences.en: It’s almost identical to the MVCCRefinement module (source, pdf), with some minor modifications to handle the new abort scenarios.
sentences.zh: 它与 MVCCRefinement 模块（源代码，pdf）几乎相同，只是做了一些小的修改来处理新的中止场景。

blocks.html_en: The main difference is that now the refinement mapping should actually hold, because SSI ensures serializability! I wasn’t able to find a counterexample when I ran the model checker against the refinement mapping, so that gave me some confidence in my model. Of course, that doesn’t prove that my implementation is correct. But it’s good enough for a learning exercise.

sentences.en: The main difference is that now the refinement mapping should actually hold, because SSI ensures serializability!
sentences.zh: 主要区别在于，现在细化映射实际上应该成立，因为 SSI 确保了可串行性！

sentences.en: I wasn’t able to find a counterexample when I ran the model checker against the refinement mapping, so that gave me some confidence in my model.
sentences.zh: 当我针对细化映射运行模型检查器时，我无法找到反例，因此这给了我对模型的信心。

sentences.en: Of course, that doesn’t prove that my implementation is correct.
sentences.zh: 当然，这并不能证明我的实现是正确的。

sentences.en: But it’s good enough for a learning exercise.
sentences.zh: 但这对于学习练习来说已经足够了。

blocks.html_en: Coda: on extending TLA+ specifications

sentences.en: Coda: on extending TLA+ specifications
sentences.zh: Coda：关于扩展 TLA+ 规范

blocks.html_en: Serializable Snapshot Isolation provides us with a nice example of when we can extend an existing specification rather than create a new one from scratch.

sentences.en: Serializable Snapshot Isolation provides us with a nice example of when we can extend an existing specification rather than create a new one from scratch.
sentences.zh: 可序列化快照隔离为我们提供了一个很好的示例，说明我们何时可以扩展现有规范而不是从头开始创建新规范。

blocks.html_en: Even so, it’s still a fair amount of work to extend an existing specification. I suspect it would have been less work to take a copy-paste-and-modify approach rather than extending it. Still, I found it a useful exercise in learning how to modify a specification by extending it.

sentences.en: Even so, it’s still a fair amount of work to extend an existing specification.
sentences.zh: 即便如此，扩展现有规范仍然需要大量工作。

sentences.en: I suspect it would have been less work to take a copy-paste-and-modify approach rather than extending it.
sentences.zh: 我怀疑采用复制粘贴和修改方法而不是扩展它会减少工作量。

sentences.en: Still, I found it a useful exercise in learning how to modify a specification by extending it.
sentences.zh: 尽管如此，我发现它对于学习如何通过扩展来修改规范来说是一个有用的练习。

content_html_zh:
在上一篇博文中，我们了解了基于多版本并发控制 (MVCC) 构建的事务隔离策略如何未实现可序列化隔离级别。 相反，它实现了一个较弱的隔离级别，称为快照隔离。 在这篇文章中，我将基于 Michael Cahill、Uwe Röhm 和 Alan Fekete 发表的工作讨论如何扩展 MVCC 模型以实现可串行化。

您可以在 SSI 模块中的 https://github.com/lorin/snapshot-isolation-tla 存储库中找到我编写的模型（源代码，pdf）。

关于约定的快速说明

在这篇文章中，我将 x=1 的读取表示为 r[x,1]。 这意味着事务读取了返回值 1 的对象 x。 正如我在上一篇文章中提到的，您可以将读取想象为以下 SQL 语句：

SELECT v FROM obj WHERE k='x';

类似地，我将 y←2 的写入表示为 w[y,2]。 这意味着事务写入了值为 2 的对象 y。 您可以将其想象为：

UPDATE obj SET v=2 WHERE k='y';

最后，我假设有一个初始事务 (T0)，它将所有对象的值设置为 0，并且在任何其他事务开始之前已提交。

背景

SQL隔离级别和现象

ANSI/ISO SQL 标准定义了四种事务隔离级别：未提交读、已提交读、可重复读和可序列化。 该标准根据隔离级别所防止的现象来定义隔离级别。 例如，脏读现象是指一个事务读取了并发事务尚未提交的写操作。 现象是危险的，因为它们可能违反软件开发人员对数据库行为方式的假设，导致软件行为不正确。

标准和新隔离级别的问题

贝伦森等人。指出该标准的措辞含糊不清，并且在定义的两种可能解释中，一种是不正确的（允许无效的执行历史），另一种过于严格（禁止有效的执行历史）。

过于严格的定义隐含地假设并发控制将使用锁定来实现，这排除了基于替代方案的有效实现，特别是多版本并发控制。 他们还提出了一种新的隔离级别：快照隔离。

形式化现象和反依赖性

在他的博士论文中，Adya 引入了一种新的形式化来推理事务隔离。 形式主义基于事务之间的直接依赖关系图。

Adya 引入的一种依赖类型称为反依赖，这对于快照隔离和可序列化之间的区别至关重要。

两个并发事务之间的反依赖关系是当一个事务读取一个对象而另一个事务以不同的值写入该对象时，例如：

据说 T1 对 T2 具有反依赖性：在序列化中，T1 必须位于 T2 之前：

在依赖图中，反依赖关系用 rw 标记，因为执行读取的事务必须在执行写入的事务之前排序，如上所示。

Adya 证明，对于支持快照隔离以生成不可序列化的执行历史记录的实现，依赖关系图中必须存在包含反依赖关系的循环。

快照隔离中的不可序列化执行历史

Fekete 等人在论文《使快照隔离可串行化》中。通过证明以下定理，进一步缩小了快照隔离可能导致不可序列化执行历史记录的条件：

定理 2.1。 假设 H 是在快照隔离下生成的不可序列化的多版本历史记录。 那么序列化图 DSG(H) 中至少有一个周期，并且我们声称在每个周期中存在三个连续的事务 Ti.1、Ti.2、Ti.3（其中 Ti.1 和 Ti.3 可能是同一个事务），使得 Ti.1 和 Ti.2 是并发的，边有 Ti.1 → Ti.2，并且 Ti.2 和 Ti.3 并发有边 Ti.2 → Ti.3。

他们还指出：

根据引理 2.3，断言存在的两个并发边必须是反依赖关系：Ti.1→Ti.2 和 Ti.2→Ti.3。

这意味着以下两种模式之一必须始终存在于不可序列化的快照隔离历史记录中：

修改 MVCC 以避免不可序列化的历史记录

卡希尔等人。提出了对 MVCC 的修改，可以动态识别可能导致不可序列化历史记录的潜在问题事务，并中止它们。 通过中止这些事务，生成的算法保证了可串行化。

正如 Fekete 等人。事实证明，在快照隔离下，只有存在包含传入反依赖边缘和传出反依赖边缘的事务（他们称之为枢轴事务）时，才会发生循环。

他们的方法是识别并中止枢轴事务：如果活动事务同时包含传出和传入反依赖项，则该事务将被中止。 请注意，这是一个保守的算法：它中止的一些事务可能仍然导致可序列化的执行历史记录。 但它确实保证了可串行性。

他们对 MVCC 的修改涉及一些额外的簿记：

  1. 每个事务执行的读取
  2. 哪些交易具有传出反依赖关系
  3. 哪些交易具有传入反依赖关系

读取的跟踪对于识别反依赖性的存在是必要的，因为反依赖性总是涉及读取（传出依赖性边缘）和写入（传入依赖性边缘）。

扩展我们的 MVCC TLA+ 模型以实现可序列化

添加变量

我创建了一个名为 SSI 的新模块，它代表可串行化快照隔离。 我扩展了 MVCC 模型，添加了三个变量，以实现 Cahill 等人要求的额外记账功能。算法。 MVCC 已经跟踪每个事务写入了哪些对象，但我们现在还需要跟踪读取。

  • rds – 哪些事务读取哪些对象
  • outc – 具有出站反依赖性的事务集
  • inc – 具有入站反依赖性的事务集

TLA+ 是无类型的（除非您使用 Apalache），但我们可以通过定义类型不变量（上面称为 TypeOkS）来表示类型信息。 定义它对于读者来说很有用，而且因为我们可以使用 TLC 模型检查器来检查它是否成立。

行为变化：新的中止机会

以下是 MVCC 中的 Next 操作与 SSI 中的等效操作的比较。

注意：由于扩展 MVCC 模块会将所有 MVCC 名称纳入范围，因此我必须为 SSI 中的每个等效操作创建新名称，我通过附加 S（例如 StartTransactionS、DeadlockDetectionS）来完成此操作。

在我们最初的 MVCC 实现中，读取和提交总是成功的。 现在，尝试读取或尝试提交也可能导致中止，因此我们需要为此采取一项操作，我将其称为 AbortRdS。

提交现在也可能会失败，因此我们现在不再采用单步 Commit 操作，而是采用 BeginCommit 操作，该操作将通过 EndCommit 操作成功完成，或者通过 AbortCommit 操作中止而失败。 由于可能引入枢轴事务，写入现在也可以中止。

使用模型检查器查找中止

以下是我如何使用 TLC 模型检查器生成新中止行为的见证：

中止读取

为了让模型检查器生成中止读取的跟踪，我在 MCSSI.tla 文件中定义了以下不变量：

然后我将其指定为不变量以检查 MCSSI.cfg 文件中的模型检查器：

INVARIANT 
    NeverAbortsRead

由于中止读取确实可能发生，因此模型检查器返回错误，并显示以下错误跟踪：

生成的跟踪如下所示，其中红色箭头表示反依赖关系。

中止提交

类似地，我们可以通过指定以下不变量，使用模型检查器来识别提交失败的场景：

检查器发现以下违反该不变量的情况：

当 T2 正在提交过程中时，T1 执行读取操作，将 T2 转变为枢轴事务。 这会导致 T2 中止。

使用细化映射检查可串行性

就像我们之前对 MVCC 所做的那样，我们可以定义从 SSI 规范到可串行化规范的细化映射。 您可以在 SSIRefinement 模块中找到它（源代码，pdf）。 它与 MVCCRefinement 模块（源代码，pdf）几乎相同，只是做了一些小的修改来处理新的中止场景。

主要区别在于，现在细化映射实际上应该成立，因为 SSI 确保了可串行性！ 当我针对细化映射运行模型检查器时，我无法找到反例，因此这给了我对模型的信心。 当然，这并不能证明我的实现是正确的。 但这对于学习练习来说已经足够了。

Coda：关于扩展 TLA+ 规范

可序列化快照隔离为我们提供了一个很好的示例，说明我们何时可以扩展现有规范而不是从头开始创建新规范。

即便如此，扩展现有规范仍然需要大量工作。 我怀疑采用复制粘贴和修改方法而不是扩展它会减少工作量。 尽管如此，我发现它对于学习如何通过扩展来修改规范来说是一个有用的练习。
